summary: The radiopaque mass suggests longstanding inflammation consistent with chronic apical periodontitis due to persistent dental decay despite no current pain or other symptomatic issues. \boxed{\text{Chronic Apical Periodontitis}} This condition results from prolonged irritation by the infected pulp material leading to calcific deposition forming the observed radiopacity near the apex. Given these factors, it aligns closely as the diagnosis for such findings.
